records.notes: Added from verified-unadded rows hei_unadded_0416 through hei_unadded_0418 as one entity to avoid V2/V3 duplication.

records.summary: DEX / exchange protocol candidate represented by CoinGecko and DefiLlama source rows. HEI treats Bulla and Bulla Exchange rows as one protocol-level entity for v0.
records.notes: Added from verified-unadded rows hei_unadded_0419 and hei_unadded_0420 as one entity. This record should be improved later with stronger launch, chain, or operating-history evidence.

records.summary: DEX / exchange protocol candidate represented by CoinPaprika, CoinGecko, and DefiLlama source rows. HEI treats Butter.xyz rows as one protocol-level entity for v0.
records.notes: Added from verified-unadded rows hei_unadded_0426 through hei_unadded_0428 as one entity. This record should be improved later with stronger launch, chain, or operating-history evidence.

records.summary: Centralized crypto exchange represented by CoinPaprika and CoinGecko source rows. HEI consolidates the related BYDFi rows into one active exchange entity.
records.notes: Added from verified-unadded rows hei_unadded_0436 through hei_unadded_0438 as one entity. Improve later with stronger launch or operating-history evidence.

records.summary: DEX / exchange protocol candidate represented by CoinGecko and DefiLlama source rows. HEI treats Byreal source rows as one protocol-level entity for v0.
records.notes: Added from verified-unadded rows hei_unadded_0439 and hei_unadded_0440 as one entity. This record should be improved later with stronger launch, chain, or operating-history evidence.

records.summary: Centralized crypto exchange candidate represented by a CoinGecko source row and official domain reference. HEI records Byte Exchange as an active exchange seed record pending stronger launch and operating-history evidence.
records.notes: Added from verified-unadded row hei_unadded_0441. This record should be improved later with stronger launch, jurisdiction, or operating-history evidence.

records.summary: DEX / exchange protocol candidate represented by CoinGecko, CoinPaprika, and DefiLlama source rows. HEI treats Camelot, Camelot V2, and Camelot V3 rows as one protocol-level entity for v0.
records.notes: Added from verified-unadded rows hei_unadded_0446 through hei_unadded_0450 as one entity. V2/V3 rows are treated as variants rather than separate deployments in v0. This record should be improved later with stronger launch, chain, version, or operating-history evidence.

records.summary: South Korean centralized cryptocurrency exchange formally launched on 2018-04-30. Coinbit announced a wind-down in November 2023 and ended trading and withdrawal support on 2023-12-29.
records.notes: The closure notice described a planned change in business direction.

records.summary: Global centralized cryptocurrency exchange founded in 2017. HEI records CoinEx as an active exchange with event-backed history, including a 2023 New York regulatory settlement and a 2023 hot-wallet security breach.
records.notes: Added from verified-unadded rows hei_unadded_0550 and hei_unadded_0551 after separate event-backed review. CoinEx remains active, so this is not a shutdown record. The 2023 hot-wallet breach event should be improved later with stronger primary or archived official-source evidence.

records.summary: Estonia-based crypto lending and exchange-service platform that halted user operations after an Estonian court-related insolvency action in 2023.
records.notes: Included because CoinLoan offered crypto exchange services in addition to lending and interest accounts.

records.summary: Cryptocurrency exchange launched in 2023 after Binance announced the sale of its Russian business to CommEX. CommEX later announced a staged shutdown beginning on 2024-03-25, with full website access scheduled to close on 2024-05-10.
records.notes: HEI treats CommEX as a separate exchange entity from Binance because it operated under a distinct CommEX brand and domain after Binance announced the sale of its Russian business. death_reason is voluntary_shutdown because available reporting describes a staged operator-announced closure rather than a hack, insolvency, or enforcement shutdown.

records.summary: Intent-based decentralized exchange and aggregator using batch auctions and a competitive solver network. CoW Swap launched on Ethereum mainnet in April 2021 and remained active after an April 2026 domain-control incident affecting its frontend domain rather than the underlying protocol systems.
records.notes: Entity-level umbrella record for the CoW Swap frontend and CoW Protocol trading system. CoW AMM remains a related product rather than a separate HEI entity in this pass.

records.summary: Solana-based decentralized exchange / liquidity protocol. HEI records Crema Finance as an active-or-research DEX record with event-backed history, centered on a July 2022 smart-contract exploit and later public reporting tying the unnamed DOJ case to Crema Finance.
records.notes: Added from verified-unadded row hei_unadded_0600 after event-backed review. The DOJ source does not name Crema Finance directly; Axios and CoinDesk are used to connect the public case and exploit reporting to Crema Finance. Improve later if archived official Crema Finance incident posts are found.

records.summary: Decentralized exchange protocol focused on stable-value and correlated-asset liquidity. Curve remains operational through its official application, documentation, governance, and open-source contract ecosystem.
records.notes: The launch date is an approximate month-level marker. HEI models Curve as one entity rather than splitting pools, chains, or contract generations.

records.summary: Crypto derivatives exchange focused on options and futures markets. HEI records Deribit as an active exchange with event-backed history, including Coinbase's 2025 agreement to acquire Deribit in a large cash-and-stock transaction.
records.notes: Added from verified-unadded rows hei_unadded_0707 and hei_unadded_0708 after event-backed review. Deribit remains active; this record does not mark it as closed. Related future Coinbase/Deribit product rows should not be split without a methodology decision.

records.summary: Decentralized exchange and on-chain liquidity protocol built around the Proactive Market Maker model. DODO V1 emerged in August 2020, the protocol expanded through later versions, and the wider service remained active after a March 2021 security incident affecting several V2 Crowdpool pools.
records.notes: Entity-level umbrella record for DODO protocol versions and supported-chain deployments. The launch marker is month-level because official documentation identifies August 2020 without asserting a stronger exact day.

records.summary: Decentralized derivatives exchange protocol whose first perpetual market launched on Ethereum on 2020-04-20. The protocol later moved from the StarkEx-based v3 deployment to the independent Cosmos-SDK-based dYdX Chain, whose mainnet launched on 2023-10-26 and reached full-production trading in November 2023.
records.notes: Entity-level umbrella record. HEI uses the first decentralized perpetual-market launch as the operational exchange start marker. v3 and dYdX Chain are modeled as lifecycle deployments of the same protocol identity rather than separate entities.

records.summary: Early Ethereum-based decentralized token exchange. HEI records EtherDelta as an inactive historical DEX with event-backed regulatory history, including the SEC's 2018 action involving its founder.
records.notes: Added from verified-unadded row hei_unadded_0843 after event-backed review. EtherDelta is treated as one historical DEX entity, not as a generic active database row. Status is inactive_or_unverified because the original venue is no longer treated as a current active exchange in this registry context.

records.summary: Singapore-based centralized cryptocurrency exchange offering spot and futures trading. Fairdesk stated that it launched in 2021 and announced in October 2024 that changing market conditions and regulatory changes had led it to permanently close. Trading positions were scheduled to close on 2024-10-17, withdrawals remained available during the wind-down, and final platform closure was set for 2024-11-30.
records.notes: Launch date is year-level and stored as 2021-01-01 for schema consistency. death_reason is voluntary_shutdown because the operator announced an orderly permanent closure; regulatory change was one stated factor rather than a documented enforcement order.

records.summary: Russia-linked centralized cryptocurrency exchange sanctioned by the United States in 2022 and later forced to suspend services in March 2025 after EU sanctions, Tether wallet blocking, and international law-enforcement action against its web infrastructure.
records.notes: Death date follows the March 2025 service suspension and seizure period. A later Grinex-related customer-base transition is not modeled here as a clean successor because public claims about continuity are disputed.

records.summary: Decentralized leveraged-trading and perpetuals exchange developed by Gains Network. gTrade has operated since 2021 and remains available through its official trading interface, documentation, and open-source repositories.
records.notes: The launch date is a year-level marker based on official materials stating operation since 2021. HEI records the exchange product as gTrade and retains Gains Network as an alias.

records.summary: Hong Kong virtual-asset trading platform operated by BX Services Limited. HKVAEX launched in February 2023, withdrew its Hong Kong VATP licence application on 2024-03-28, and completed a phased shutdown on 2024-05-01.
records.notes: The February launch date is month-level. HEI does not assert disputed ownership claims.

records.summary: South Korean centralized cryptocurrency exchange established in 2017 as the Korean arm of Huobi/HTX. Huobi Korea separated its management and ownership relationship from the global exchange in January 2023, then ended virtual-asset exchange services on 2024-01-29 because of a difficult business environment.
records.notes: HEI uses voluntary_shutdown because the exchange's announcement framed the closure as a business-environment decision. The former official domain now serves unrelated Korean content and is treated as repurposed.

records.summary: Solana-based decentralized trading platform that began as a liquidity-routing swap aggregator and expanded into an exchange product suite including perpetual futures. HEI models Jupiter as one entity rather than splitting its swap, limit-order, DCA, and perpetual products into separate exchanges.
records.notes: Launch date is month-level and represents the public Jupiter exchange/aggregator era. Product modules and versions are not separate HEI entities.

records.summary: Ukrainian centralized cryptocurrency exchange that announced an orderly shutdown in 2025 after domestic access was blocked during an investigation and regulatory/legal pressure in Ukraine.
records.notes: Death date follows the announced complete platform cessation date of 2025-04-30. The shutdown was presented as orderly, but the underlying trigger involved court-ordered blocking and Bureau of Economic Security investigation context, so death_reason is classified as regulation.

records.summary: Decentralized exchange and liquidity-aggregation protocol originating from Kyber Network. KyberSwap evolved through Classic and Elastic protocol generations and remained active after a November 2023 security incident affecting Elastic liquidity pools.
records.notes: Entity-level umbrella record for KyberSwap's interface, aggregator and liquidity protocols. KyberDMM and KyberSwap Elastic are treated as lifecycle versions, not separate entities.

records.summary: Decentralized exchange launched on Avalanche under the Trader Joe brand and later renamed LFJ. The protocol expanded beyond its original chain and operates a multichain DEX and trading platform centered on Liquidity Book infrastructure.
records.notes: Launch date is month-level for the original Trader Joe DEX. LFJ is modeled as the continuing identity of the same protocol rather than a separate successor entity.

records.summary: Japan-focused crypto asset exchange service operated within the LINE group. LVC launched BITMAX on 2019-09-17 as a registered Japanese crypto-asset exchange accessible through the LINE app. LINE Xenesis later announced that LINE BITMAX would end on 2026-06-01 and that the company would discontinue its crypto-asset exchange business. The service site and help center now state that the service ended on that date.
records.notes: This is LINE's Japan-market BITMAX service, not the unrelated BitMax.io / AscendEX exchange. death_reason is voluntary_shutdown because the operator formally chose to end the service while citing market changes, competition, and resource allocation.

records.summary: Decentralized exchange aggregator built by 0x Labs and launched in June 2020. Matcha searches and routes orders across multiple decentralized liquidity sources while presenting an end-user, non-custodial trading interface.
records.notes: Modeled as a distinct end-user DEX aggregator built by 0x, not as a duplicate of the underlying 0x Protocol infrastructure.

records.summary: Decentralized exchange candidate identified in the HEI verified-unadded backlog from CoinPaprika and CoinGecko source data and currently treated as an active DEX record.
records.notes: Added from verified-unadded backlog rows hei_unadded_0018 and hei_unadded_0019. Source evidence is limited to CoinPaprika and CoinGecko database references, so this record should be improved later if stronger official-history evidence is found. File slug is normalized as nine-inch while preserving 9inch as canonical_name.

records.summary: Decentralized exchange candidate identified in the HEI verified-unadded backlog from CoinPaprika, CoinGecko, and DefiLlama source data and currently treated as an active DEX record.
records.notes: Added from verified-unadded backlog rows hei_unadded_0020 through hei_unadded_0023. Source evidence is limited to CoinPaprika, CoinGecko, and DefiLlama database references, so this record should be improved later if stronger official-history evidence is found. File slug is normalized as nine-mm while preserving 9mm as canonical_name.

records.summary: Decentralized exchange candidate identified in the HEI verified-unadded backlog from CoinGecko and DefiLlama source data and currently treated as an active DEX record.
records.notes: Added from verified-unadded backlog rows hei_unadded_0008 and hei_unadded_0009. Source evidence is limited to CoinGecko and DefiLlama database references, so this record should be improved later if stronger official-history evidence is found.

records.summary: Decentralized exchange aggregation and DeFi swap protocol ecosystem that routes token swaps across decentralized liquidity sources and remains active under the 1inch brand.
records.notes: Added from verified-unadded backlog row hei_unadded_0010. The original 1inch.io domain now redirects to 1inch.com. Launch date is stored at month precision using the commonly reported May 2019 ETHGlobal prototype origin; HEI should later refine this if official launch-date evidence is added.

records.summary: Solana-based decentralized exchange protocol centered on automated market-making and concentrated-liquidity pools. Orca remains operational through its official application, developer documentation, and open-source repositories.
records.notes: The launch date is an approximate month-level marker. HEI treats Orca as one entity and does not split individual pools or program generations.

records.summary: App-chain decentralized exchange and cross-chain liquidity hub in the Cosmos ecosystem. Osmosis remains operational through its official trading interface, documentation, governance, and open-source chain software.
records.notes: HEI models Osmosis as one exchange entity rather than splitting its chain, pools, and interfaces.

records.summary: South Korean centralized exchange operated by Oceans Co., Ltd. ProBit Korea opened its Korean-won service on 2019-01-29 and ended exchange services on 2024-03-16.
records.notes: Modeled separately from ProBit Global. The launch marker is the opening of Korean-won deposits.

records.summary: Decentralized exchange protocol launched for the Polygon ecosystem and later expanded across EVM networks. QuickSwap remains operational through its official exchange interface, documentation, and open-source repositories.
records.notes: The official documentation describes an October 2021 launch. HEI keeps versions and chain deployments under one entity.

records.summary: Solana-based decentralized exchange and liquidity protocol that began operating in 2021 as an AMM integrated with the Serum/OpenBook order-book ecosystem. Raydium remained active after a December 2022 pool-authority compromise and subsequently moved administrative authority to multisig controls.
records.notes: Entity-level umbrella record for Raydium's Solana DEX, liquidity and launch products. The launch marker is year-level because the official documentation states that Raydium started in 2021 without asserting a stronger exact day.

records.summary: Solana-based non-custodial decentralized exchange and shared central-limit-order-book protocol launched in August 2020. Serum became unsafe to rely on after the November 2022 FTX security incident because FTX retained the program upgrade authority. Solana ecosystem developers deployed the community-controlled OpenBook fork, and Project Serum stated on 2022-11-29 that the original Serum mainnet program had become defunct.
records.notes: HEI uses death_reason hack because the terminal abandonment of the original program followed the FTX security incident and loss of trust in FTX-controlled upgrade authority. This does not assert a direct loss from the Serum order book; the decisive issue was that program control could no longer be treated as secure. OpenBook is recorded as the community fork and functional replacement, but successor_id remains null until OpenBook has its own canonical HEI entity.

records.summary: Aptos-based decentralized exchange that launched in June 2025 and entered a phased voluntary shutdown in May 2026. Tapp Exchange announced on 2026-05-01 that the protocol would remain available through 2026-05-31, after which its frontend would be taken offline and remaining withdrawals would require direct interaction with on-chain smart contracts.
records.notes: Launch date is normalized to 2025-06-01 because contemporary shutdown reporting establishes June 2025 but not an exact launch day. The dead classification records the official end of the operated protocol frontend and normal service; immutable smart contracts may remain accessible for direct withdrawals. death_reason is voluntary_shutdown because the team announced a staged wind-down without reporting a hack or insolvency as the cause.

records.summary: Cross-chain decentralized liquidity protocol and exchange network launched as multichain Chaosnet on 2021-04-13. THORChain remained active after two Ethereum-router exploits in July 2021, when trading was halted, liquidity providers were promised reimbursement, and the protocol introduced additional security controls before returning to operation.
records.notes: Entity-level umbrella record for the THORChain network rather than individual frontends or chain integrations.

records.summary: UK-registered centralized digital-asset exchange whose corporate and regulatory position deteriorated sharply in 2026. OFAC designated Zedxion Exchange Ltd on 2026-01-30 for operating in Iran's financial sector and for materially supporting the IRGC. Companies House later dissolved the UK company through compulsory strike-off on 2026-05-12 after identifying misleading, false, or deceptive incorporation information. HEI classifies Zedxion as limited rather than dead because its public exchange website remained accessible and continued to advertise registration and trading after the corporate dissolution date.
records.notes: The launch date uses the UK entity's incorporation date as the earliest authoritative public-lineage marker; an independently verified exact trading-launch date has not been established. status is limited because the legal entity was dissolved and the exchange was designated by OFAC, but the public website still presented active exchange services as of verification. death_reason and death_date remain null until operational cessation is directly established.

records.summary: Decentralized exchange liquidity and swap infrastructure protocol that aggregates and routes token swaps across on-chain liquidity sources and remains active under the 0x brand.
records.notes: Added from verified-unadded backlog row hei_unadded_0002. Slug is normalized as zero-x-protocol to avoid path ambiguity while preserving 0x as canonical alias. Launch date is left null pending stronger date-specific evidence.

records.summary: Centralized digital asset exchange brand whose Thailand exchange and broker licenses were revoked in 2024 after regulatory suspension and unresolved operating issues.
records.notes: Terminal marker follows Thailand license revocation.

records.title: Bankruptcy proceedings commenced
records.description: Tokyo District Court commenced bankruptcy proceedings for MtGox Co., Ltd. on April 24, 2014.

records.title: Chapter 11 filing and collapse
records.description: FTX and affiliated debtors filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection on November 11, 2022.

records.title: Binance launched
records.description: Binance began operating as a centralized exchange.

records.title: CCAA protection and insolvency crisis
records.description: Quadriga sought and received CCAA protection on February 5, 2019 after the platform had shut down on January 28, 2019.

records.title: FinCEN penalties and U.S. enforcement action
records.description: FinCEN assessed penalties against BTC-e on July 26, 2017 as U.S. enforcement action escalated.

records.title: January 2019 exchange hack
records.description: Official liquidation materials describe a serious January 2019 hack that caused severe operational and reputational damage.
records.notes: The official source confirms the hack occurred in January 2019, but not the precise day.

records.title: Liquidators appointed and trading suspended
records.description: Cryptopia reached an operational end state associated with hack.

records.title: Operations wound down
records.description: Global exchange brand from the broader Bittrex lineage, later wound down after regulatory pressure and liquidation proceedings.

records.title: Operations ceased
records.description: Centralized exchange founded in 2018 that ceased CEX operations in May 2023 after deteriorating operating conditions and earlier disruption tied to an investigation.

records.title: Ownership changed through acquisition
records.description: Poloniex underwent an acquisition-related ownership change.
records.notes: Ownership transition only; exchange continued operating.

records.title: Vaultoro launched
records.description: Vaultoro began operating as a centralized exchange.

records.title: Platform migration underway
records.description: Official messaging described a temporary migration with customer assets stated to remain safe.
records.notes: Official site showed a migration/update notice in April 2026 with customer assets stated to remain safe. Launch date uses the May 2015 public launch reference for the trading service; company/about material also claims operations since 2014.

records.title: Cryptsy launched
records.description: Cryptsy began operating as a centralized exchange; launch date precision is currently limited to month level.

records.title: Insolvency crisis became definitive
records.description: Cryptsy reached its terminal operating state on the date used by HEI as the end marker.
records.notes: Later reporting places the exchange launch in May 2013. The operational end marker uses the January 2016 disclosure of hidden losses and withdrawal failure.

records.title: BitGrail launched
records.description: BitGrail began operating as a centralized exchange; launch date precision is currently limited to year level.

records.title: Operations ceased
records.description: BitGrail ceased operations after the crisis that ultimately ended the platform.
records.notes: Founding year is treated as 2017 from retrospective reporting. Death marker uses the Florence court order to cease operations on 2018-05-03; the primary collapse trigger remained the February 2018 Nano loss.

records.title: Operations ceased
records.description: Livecoin ceased operations after the crisis that ultimately ended the platform.
records.notes: Launch uses the specific April 2015 operating-since statement from the shutdown coverage. Some secondary profiles cite earlier founding dates; these are left in notes rather than used as the operational launch marker.

records.title: Operations ceased
records.description: MapleChange ceased operations after the crisis that ultimately ended the platform.
records.notes: Public reporting conflicts on whether the terminal crisis is better characterized as a hack or an exit scam.

records.title: Cobinhood platform went live
records.description: Cobinhood began operating as a centralized cryptocurrency exchange platform.
records.notes: HEI uses the 2017-12-18 live-platform report as the launch marker.

records.title: Cobinhood was moved to dead-side handling after shutdown notice
records.description: Cobinhood reached its terminal HEI operating state after the 2020-01-10 shutdown and account-balance audit notice.
records.notes: The notice described an audit period and later reopening for users to retrieve funds; HEI uses the first shutdown notice as the terminal marker while keeping the cause unknown.

records.title: Altsbit launched
records.description: Altsbit began operating as a centralized exchange; launch date precision is currently limited to month level.

records.title: Operations ceased
records.description: Altsbit ceased operations after the crisis that ultimately ended the platform.
records.notes: Public coverage indicates the venue was only a few months old by February 2020. The operational end marker uses the published refund deadline in May 2020.

records.title: Operations wound down
records.description: WEX reached its terminal operating state on the date used by HEI as the end marker.
records.notes: Launch uses the September 2017 rebrand/launch announcement. Death marker uses the late-November 2018 domain-loss point as an operational end marker; exact final shutdown timing remains contested.

records.title: Operations wound down
records.description: BX Thailand reached its terminal operating state on the date used by HEI as the end marker.

records.title: Operations interrupted
records.description: COSS went offline and then entered an acquisition/relaunch process, leaving long-term status ambiguous.
records.notes: COSS was taken offline on 2020-01-07 and later described acquisition negotiations and a relaunch plan. Because the record is too ambiguous for a clean dead-side classification, it is moved to inactive pending deeper verification.

records.title: Vircurex launched
records.description: Vircurex began operating as a centralized exchange; launch date precision is currently limited to month level.

records.title: Insolvency crisis became definitive
records.description: Vircurex reached its terminal operating state on the date used by HEI as the end marker.
records.notes: Launch uses repeated October 2011 references. Operational end marker uses the March 2014 withdrawal freeze and account-freeze period, which followed earlier hacks and a reserve crisis.

records.title: Operations ceased
records.description: CoinsMarkets ceased operations after the crisis that ultimately ended the platform.
records.notes: Launch remains approximate because strong primary founding records are still missing. Operational end marker uses the point when users widely reported trading and withdrawals disabled on 2018-01-05.

records.title: MintPal launched
records.description: MintPal began operating as a centralized exchange.

records.title: Operations ceased
records.description: MintPal ceased operations after the crisis that ultimately ended the platform.
records.notes: Launch uses a specific February 2014 launch reference. Death marker uses 2014-10-14 when Moolah announced MintPal had been shut down while preparing bankruptcy; later fallout continued after that date.

records.title: Vault of Satoshi launched
records.description: Vault of Satoshi began operating as a centralized exchange.

records.title: Operations wound down
records.description: Vault of Satoshi reached its terminal operating state on the date used by HEI as the end marker.
records.notes: Launch uses October 2013 reporting on the exchange going live. Closure date and reason come from the January 2015 shutdown announcement specifying a voluntary end rather than insolvency or theft.

records.title: Poloniex spins out from Circle
records.description: Poloniex leadership spun the exchange out of Circle into Polo Digital Assets, Ltd., continuing operations outside Circle.
records.notes: Ownership / corporate structure change; not a death-side terminal event.

records.title: Trading and wallet use suspended
records.description: LocalBitcoins suspended trading and normal wallet use on 2023-02-16, leaving withdrawals only afterward.
records.notes: Closure was announced on 2023-02-09; this event uses the effective service-stop date.

records.title: Kraken acquisition announced
records.description: Kraken announced the Coinsetter acquisition on 2016-01-19 and stated that client accounts would be transferred on 2016-01-26.
records.notes: Entity death_date is tracked separately as the 2016-01-26 transfer date.

records.title: Kraken acquisition announced
records.description: Kraken announced the Cavirtex acquisition on 2016-01-19 and stated that client accounts would be transferred on 2016-01-26.
records.notes: Entity death_date is tracked separately as the 2016-01-26 transfer date.

records.title: BitBay rebranded to Zonda
records.description: BitBay announced on 2021-11-08 that the exchange brand had changed to Zonda, with legacy BitBay platform URLs later redirecting to Zonda domains.
records.notes: Further downstream rebranding to zondacrypto should be handled as successor lineage, not silent overwrite.

records.title: BITFRONT service terminated
records.description: BITFRONT's closure notice wound down service in stages and ended withdrawals and remaining customer access on 2023-03-31.
records.notes: The initial shutdown announcement was published on 2022-11-28.

records.title: OPNX ceased operations
records.description: OPNX told users to withdraw funds and export historical data by 2024-02-14 as part of its February 2024 shutdown.
records.notes: Shutdown date is supported by strong reporting; launch lineage traces to the March 2023 CoinFLEX-to-OPNX rebrand.

records.title: Youbit halted trading and filed for bankruptcy
records.description: Youbit stopped trading and entered bankruptcy proceedings after disclosing a second 2017 hack that reduced customer balances.
records.notes: The retained marker combines the service halt and bankruptcy filing date used in same-day reporting.

records.title: Gatecoin ordered to wind up and cease operations
records.description: Gatecoin ceased operations after a Hong Kong court granted a winding-up order against the company, leading to compulsory liquidation.
records.notes: Liquidators were formally appointed on 2019-03-20; HEI keeps 2019-03-13 as the operational stop and terminal marker.

records.title: Koinex permanently disabled trading
records.description: Koinex permanently disabled digital-asset trading on all platforms on 2019-06-27 and moved to withdrawals-only wind-down.
records.notes: The later withdrawal deadline of 2019-07-15 is kept as supporting context, not the entity death marker.

records.title: CoinCorner acquired Coinfloor customer base and domains
records.description: CoinCorner acquired Coinfloor's customer base and domains in October 2021 and began migrating or closing legacy Coinfloor accounts.
records.notes: Further lineage work should attach the successor relationship once CoinCorner is modeled in canonical data.

records.title: Kraken acquisition announced
records.description: Kraken announced the CleverCoin acquisition on 2016-06-28 and said customer accounts would be automatically transferred the next day.
records.notes: Entity death_date is tracked separately as the 2016-06-29 transfer completion date.

records.title: Coinnest shutdown process began
records.description: Coinnest posted a closure notice saying it was no longer operating as of Tuesday and set an April 30 deadline for withdrawals.
records.notes: The retained event date follows the effective service-stop marker referenced in reporting on April 18–19, 2019.

records.title: Liqui announced shutdown and 30 day withdrawal window
records.description: Liqui announced it was shutting down on 2019-01-28 and told users they had 30 days to withdraw funds.
records.notes: Shutdown rationale quoted in reporting focused on insufficient liquidity and lack of economic justification for continued service.

records.title: Nova ceased trading and API access
records.description: Nova said trading would cease and the API would be deactivated on 2019-10-07 as part of its shutdown schedule.
records.notes: Withdrawals were left open until 2019-10-25, but the operational end marker used here is the trading/API stop date.

records.title: Interim receiver secured Einstein Exchange
records.description: The Supreme Court of British Columbia appointed an interim receiver on 2019-11-01, which then secured the premises and shut down platform access.
records.notes: BCSC said platform counsel had told regulators on 2019-10-31 that the business planned to shut down within 30 to 60 days due to a lack of profit.

records.title: The Rock Trading interrupted platform operations
records.description: The Rock Trading said it had to interrupt operation of its platform because of difficulties encountered in liquidity management.
records.notes: The operational stop preceded judicial liquidation and insolvency proceedings in Milan.

records.title: VirWoX permanently closed its service
records.description: VirWoX notified users in late 2019 that the service would permanently close on 2020-01-06 and account access would end that day.
records.notes: The retained date follows the stated end-of-service date rather than the earlier announcement date.

records.title: Bitcurex went offline after reported external interference
records.description: Bitcurex reported asset losses and went offline in October 2016.
records.notes: Followed by a prosecutor investigation in 2017.

records.title: Tradehill halted trading again
records.description: Tradehill halted trading in August 2013 because of banking and regulatory issues.
records.notes: Earlier 2012 shutdown remains contextual evidence.

records.title: Coin.mx operators were arrested and charged
records.description: U.S. authorities charged Coin.mx operators with running an unlawful bitcoin exchange and money-laundering scheme.
records.notes: Enforcement-side operational end marker.

records.title: Intersango closed its remaining GBP market
records.description: Intersango progressively shut markets during 2012 and closed its remaining GBP market in December.
records.notes: Uses the final GBP closure date.

records.title: Binance Uganda fully shut down
records.description: Binance Uganda announced a phased wind-down and fully shut down on 2020-11-28.
records.notes: Regional Binance surface.

records.title: Mt. Gox acquired Bitomat
records.description: After Bitomat lost wallet data and customer balances, Mt. Gox acquired the service and migrated users in August 2011.
records.notes: Successor is Mt. Gox.

records.title: Cointrader closed after alleged hack
records.description: Cointrader said it had closed its doors effective immediately after a debilitating hack.
records.notes: Immediate shutdown notice.

records.title: Yunbi announced closure of trading operations
records.description: Yunbi said it would close trading during the 2017 China crackdown on exchanges.
records.notes: China crackdown closure.

records.title: Binance Jersey fully shut down

records.title: ACH and wire transfers halted

records.title: Georgia cease and desist became final
records.description: The Georgia Department of Banking and Finance said an order to cease and desist against Bulbul Investments LLC doing business as CampBX became final on 2018-07-25.

records.title: CryptoBridge shutdown announced

records.title: Coinroom entered the KNF warning list
records.description: Coinroom was added to the Polish KNF public warning list over suspected unlicensed payment-services activity.
records.notes: Important regulatory precursor, but not the final terminal event.

records.title: Coinroom shut down
records.description: Users reported receiving account-termination notices and Coinroom ceased operating in early April 2019.
records.notes: Cause remains conservative because later reporting mixes regulatory and fund-loss frames.

records.title: Bitsane withdrawals began failing
records.description: Users reported that Bitsane withdrawals began failing in May 2019, with support citing temporary technical reasons before the exchange later disappeared.
records.notes: Start-of-failure marker uses month-level dating from aligned reporting.

records.title: Bitsane website and social accounts disappeared
records.description: By 2019-06-17, the Bitsane website and social media accounts had disappeared after weeks of withdrawal failures, and public reporting later treated the case as an apparent exit scam.
records.notes: HEI uses the 2019-06-17 disappearance date as the terminal marker while preserving the later 2019-06-27 public inactive / scam-style reporting in evidence.

records.title: Cold wallet hack
records.description: Bter said 7,170 BTC were stolen from its cold wallet and the platform was temporarily shut down.
records.notes: Major historical incident, but not the final terminal event.

records.title: Bter users migrated to Gate.io
records.description: Gate.io announced support for BTER users to move funds to gate.io, reflecting the completed brand transition.
records.notes: HEI uses 2017-12-31 as the conservative rebrand marker.

records.title: Abucoins entered the KNF warning list
records.description: The Polish KNF added Abucoins sp. z o.o. to its public warnings list over suspected unauthorized payment-services activity.
records.notes: Important regulatory precursor, not the final terminal event.

records.title: Abucoins closed down
records.description: By 2019-02-26, Abucoins was publicly marked closed and inactive.
records.notes: Uses the 2019-02-26 closure marker from public exchange-status sources.

records.title: Cryptonit marked closed

records.notes: The same closure notice also said that technical problems affected parts of the service.

records.title: Nominex announced closure of its centralized exchange
records.description: Nominex informed users by email that it was closing its centralized exchange for economic reasons and advised users to withdraw funds as soon as possible.
records.notes: The closure notice said the wind-down process could take up to four months.

records.description: Public exchange-status sources marked Nominex as dead on 2024-12-18 after the closure announcement for its centralized exchange.
records.notes: HEI uses the public dead-side marker rather than the later stages of the operational wind-down.

records.title: Coindirect announced closure of its retail service
records.description: Coindirect announced that it was closing down its retail service after five years and would focus on its B2B business instead.
records.notes: The notice instructed users to withdraw crypto and fiat assets before 2023-01-24.

records.title: Hoo Exchange was reported as failing to process withdrawals
records.description: Public exchange-status coverage stated that numerous independent sources reported that Hoo Exchange was not processing withdrawals and that accounts were being blocked without explanation.
records.notes: Cryptowisser also noted that its researchers received no response when contacting the exchange for clarification.

records.title: Hoo Exchange was marked scam and moved to dead-side handling
records.description: Public exchange-status sources marked Hoo Exchange as a scam and moved it to graveyard handling on 2022-08-17.
records.notes: HEI uses the public dead-side marker rather than later suspended-activity warnings.

records.title: BiteBTC faced widespread scam and blocked-withdrawal accusations
records.description: Public exchange-status coverage recorded extensive user accusations describing BiteBTC as a scam and reporting serious platform trust issues.
records.notes: The review also stated that the original site behavior had become unreliable and confusing.

records.title: BiteBTC was marked dead and moved to scam-side handling
records.description: Public exchange-status sources marked BiteBTC as dead on 2019-11-01 after concluding that it was widely acknowledged as a scam.
records.notes: HEI uses the public dead-side marker rather than later domain behavior.

records.title: Livecoin reported a major platform attack
records.description: Public reporting stated that Livecoin lost control over parts of its infrastructure during a late-December 2020 attack and the platform became unsafe to use.
records.notes: Cryptowisser later referenced the 23-24 December 2020 attack window when moving the exchange to dead-side handling.

records.title: Livecoin was moved to dead-side handling
records.description: Public exchange-status sources marked Livecoin as dead on 2020-12-27 after the hacker attack, before the later formal closure notice.

records.title: C2CX was moved to dead-side handling
records.description: Public exchange-status sources marked C2CX dead on 2021-05-10 after the exchange announced that it had closed down.
records.notes: HEI uses the 2021-05-10 dead-side update as the terminal marker.

records.title: Coinzo was reported to have shut down amid regulatory pressure
records.description: Public exchange-status coverage stated on 2021-10-11 that Coinzo seemed to have closed down its operations and that Turkish media had linked the shutdown to increased regulatory pressure from Turkish authorities.
records.notes: HEI uses the 2021-10-11 public update as the shutdown announcement marker.

records.title: Coinzo was moved to dead-side handling
records.description: Public exchange-status sources marked Coinzo dead on 2021-10-11 after reports that the exchange had shut down.
records.notes: HEI uses the 2021-10-11 dead-side update as the terminal marker.

records.title: Gatecoin announced winding-up and immediate cessation of operations
records.description: Public reporting reproduced Gatecoin's notice that a Hong Kong court had granted a winding-up order and that operations ceased immediately.
records.notes: Some secondary coverage appeared after the 2019-03-13 order date; HEI uses the order and immediate cessation date.

records.title: BitMarket announced closure due to loss of liquidity
records.description: BitMarket posted a notice stating that, due to loss of liquidity, Bitmarket.pl/net was forced to cease operations from 2019-07-08.
records.notes: The platform notice is quoted by multiple public sources.

records.title: BitMarket was moved to dead-side handling after liquidity failure
records.description: Public exchange-status sources marked BitMarket inactive after the platform announced that it had ceased operations because of liquidity loss.
records.notes: HEI uses the closure-notice date as the terminal marker.

records.title: Cobinhood announced shutdown and account-balance audit
records.description: Cobinhood announced that the exchange would shut down for an account-balance audit from 2020-01-10 to 2020-02-09 and warned users not to deposit assets during that period.
records.notes: The announcement said the exchange would reopen on 2020-02-10 for users to retrieve funds, but public reporting treated the situation as troubled and ambiguous.

records.title: OpenLedger DEX closure notice was published
records.description: Public exchange-status coverage recorded an OpenLedger DEX closure notice on 2020-04-25 stating that the exchange would close down as of 2020-05-15.
records.notes: HEI keeps the update date separate from the later terminal close-down date.

records.title: OpenLedger DEX closed down
records.description: Public exchange-status sources state that OpenLedger DEX closed down as of 2020-05-15 and is no longer operational.
records.notes: HEI uses the 2020-05-15 close-down date as the terminal marker.

records.title: ANXPRO announced it would not resume exchange services
records.description: Public exchange-status coverage recorded an ANXPRO website message stating that the exchange had decided not to resume digital assets exchange services.
records.notes: The public message is treated as the shutdown / non-resumption announcement marker.

records.title: ANXPRO was moved to dead-side handling
records.description: Public exchange-status sources treated ANXPRO as inactive after the exchange stated that it would not resume digital-asset exchange services.
records.notes: HEI uses the 2020-09-01 non-resumption message as the terminal marker.

records.title: Comkort announced its planned closure
records.description: Comkort announced that the exchange would cease to exist on 2015-07-19, citing lack of return during the prior year and asking customers to withdraw funds before the closure.
records.notes: The notice also stated that trading would be turned off on 2015-07-01 and that servers would be turned off on 2015-07-20.

records.title: Comkort reached its terminal closure date
records.description: Comkort's published closure notice set 2015-07-19 as the final date for users to withdraw funds before servers were scheduled to be turned off the following day.
records.notes: HEI uses the final withdrawal / closure date as the terminal marker rather than the 2015-07-20 technical server-off date.

records.title: CCEDK announced trading-engine transition
records.description: Public reporting stated that CCEDK's current trading engine would cease in May 2016 while withdrawals remained available through June and the operation prepared to relaunch in a new OpenLedger-centered form.
records.notes: The exact announcement appears across 2016-05-15 and 2016-05-16 coverage; HEI uses the Forbes publication date as the announcement marker.

records.title: CCEDK relaunched into OpenLedger-centered structure
records.description: Public reporting stated that CCEDK would relaunch on 2016-07-01 as a decentralized conglomerate and transition from hosting a traditional exchange to supporting OpenLedger as service center, fiat gateway, and crowdfunding partner.
records.notes: HEI uses 2016-07-01 as the terminal rebrand marker for the original CCEDK exchange identity.

records.title: Coin-Swap announced permanent closure
records.description: A public forum post quoted Coin-Swap.net's notice that the exchange would close permanently on 2015-03-22 and that users must withdraw all coins before that date.
records.notes: The notice date is taken from the forum timestamp that reproduced the closure notice.

records.title: Coin-Swap reached its terminal closure date
records.description: Coin-Swap.net's quoted closure notice set 2015-03-22 as the permanent shutdown date and stated that servers and the AWS account would be terminated.
records.notes: HEI uses the final withdrawal / permanent closure date as the terminal marker.

records.title: Justcoin announced shutdown after losing banking partner
records.description: Justcoin announced that it was shutting down after its banking partner terminated the relationship and the exchange could not secure an alternative banking partner in Norway.
records.notes: This was the operating halt before the later ANX acquisition and revival.

records.title: ANX acquired Justcoin domain and brand rights
records.description: ANX acquired the domain name and brand rights to the troubled Norway-based Justcoin exchange and planned to revive the exchange under new management.
records.notes: The acquisition announcement is the basis for HEI's acquired status.

records.title: Justcoin was scheduled to resume under new management
records.description: Public reporting stated that Justcoin would resume operations under new ANX management on 2014-11-24 using ANX technology.
records.notes: HEI treats this as the terminal marker for the original independent Justcoin identity.

records.title: Yacuna announced planned closure
records.description: Yacuna announced via customer email that it would close the exchange and asked customers to withdraw any remaining funds before 2015-11-15.
records.notes: The announcement was described as an orderly closure, with withdrawals processed manually in batches.

records.title: Yacuna reached its terminal closure date
records.description: Yacuna's closure notice gave 2015-11-15 as the deadline for customers to withdraw remaining funds before the exchange closed.
records.notes: HEI uses the customer withdrawal deadline / closure date as the terminal marker.

records.title: AllCrypt reported a WordPress-related compromise and BTC theft
records.description: Public reporting and security analysis described AllCrypt as having been compromised through its WordPress setup, resulting in stolen cryptocurrency and the exchange going offline.
records.notes: The reported theft amount varies slightly across sources, but the compromise and site-offline state are consistently described.

records.title: AllCrypt was moved to dead-side handling after the hack
records.description: After the March 2015 compromise, AllCrypt went offline and is now listed as inactive by exchange data references.
records.notes: HEI uses the public hack/site-offline marker as the terminal date rather than a later unstated final domain-offline date.

records.title: BitBay rebranded to Zonda
records.description: The exchange previously known as BitBay moved into the Zonda brand in November 2021, with user login data and platform continuity described as unchanged.
records.notes: Month/day marker follows public reporting of the 2021 BitBay-to-Zonda rebrand.

records.title: Zonda brand moved to zondacrypto
records.description: The Zonda exchange brand later moved to the zondacrypto name; HEI treats BitBay / Zonda / zondacrypto as the same lineage unless later evidence requires a split.
records.notes: The exact source should be upgraded to an accessible official archived page if available.

records.title: Polish prosecutors opened a Zondacrypto investigation
records.description: The Regional Prosecutor's Office in Katowice opened an investigation concerning Zondacrypto, including suspected misleading of users about the purchase and storage of fiat currencies and cryptocurrencies, possible money-laundering-related conduct, reported withdrawal problems, and lack of access to an approximately 4,500 BTC cold wallet.
records.notes: Official prosecutor material reports damage of no less than 350 million PLN and states that the investigation concerns conduct from 2022 onward.

records.title: Victim instructions referenced failed or partial withdrawal requests
records.description: The prosecutor's office published instructions for potential victims, asking them to document when and in what amount they ordered withdrawals from Zondacrypto, including withdrawals that were not fully or partially executed.
records.notes: This is modeled as withdrawal_suspended because official victim instructions and investigation materials document withdrawal failures, but HEI should avoid claiming a final global service shutdown until stronger official confirmation appears.

records.title: CEO reportedly located in Israel during crisis
records.description: Onet reported that zondacrypto CEO Przemyslaw Kral was in Israel while the exchange was described as on the edge of bankruptcy and while the 4,500 BTC reserve-key dispute remained unresolved.
records.notes: Keep this as supporting crisis context, not as the primary status-changing event.

records.title: CoinMKT public beta launched
records.description: CoinMKT launched its public beta as a United States cryptocurrency exchange supporting USD and multiple cryptocurrency markets.
records.notes: Launch marker uses CoinDesk's pre-launch coverage saying public beta would launch the following week and later exchange profile references.

records.title: ANX acquired CoinMKT domain and brand rights
records.description: ANX acquired the domain name and brand rights to CoinMKT.com, with CoinMKT continuing under the ANX umbrella using ANX technology.
records.notes: HEI treats this as the terminal acquired marker for the original independent CoinMKT identity.

records.title: McxNOW suspended trading after support overload
records.description: McxNOW announced that it would suspend trading for several months because user growth and support requirements had overwhelmed the service, with users told to withdraw funds before the temporary closure.
records.notes: This is an earlier suspension event, not the terminal marker.

records.title: McxNOW entered terminal maintenance / inactive state
records.description: Public exchange references state that McxNOW would enter a maintenance period beginning 2014-11-15, with coins not withdrawn by that date to be destroyed and wallet data deleted.
records.notes: HEI uses the maintenance / withdrawal-deadline date as the terminal marker because later market-data references list the exchange as inactive.

records.title: CoinPulse announced indefinite maintenance plan
records.description: CoinPulse published an urgent notice stating that trading and deposits would be suspended from 2019-02-01, withdrawals would remain open until 2019-02-07, and the exchange would then enter maintenance until further notice.
records.notes: The official notice also stated that CoinPulse had been working with an investor and invited offers to acquire the exchange, website, trademark, and unused CPEX tokens.

records.title: CoinPulse suspended trading and deposits
records.description: CoinPulse's official notice set 2019-02-01 as the date when all trading and deposits would be suspended while withdrawals remained open.
records.notes: This is modeled separately from the final maintenance / withdrawal-deadline marker.

records.title: CoinPulse entered terminal indefinite-maintenance state
records.description: CoinPulse's official notice said withdrawals would stay open until 2019-02-07 and that the exchange would then be placed into maintenance until further notice.
records.notes: HEI uses the withdrawal deadline / indefinite-maintenance date as the terminal marker.

records.title: Bit.com announced a phased shutdown and user asset migration plan
records.description: Bit.com began a three-phase wind-down on December 27, 2025, disabled new user registrations, and directed users to withdraw assets or migrate balances to Matrixport.
records.notes: The available evidence describes an orderly wind-down rather than an insolvency or hack event.

records.title: Bit.com spot trading scheduled to end
records.description: The wind-down plan scheduled the end of spot trading for January 31, 2026, followed by restrictions on contract trading and withdrawal-only handling.
records.notes: Trading halt marker follows the shutdown timeline reported by multiple secondary sources.

records.title: Bit.com final shutdown deadline reached
records.description: The phased shutdown plan concluded with a final migration / withdrawal deadline of March 31, 2026.
records.notes: HEI uses the final migration / withdrawal deadline as the terminal marker.

records.title: Drift Protocol suffered a major exploit
records.description: Drift Protocol confirmed an active attack on April 1, 2026; public estimates ranged from over $130 million to about $285 million, with later analysis describing privileged-access and fake-token collateral mechanisms.
records.notes: Use exploit rather than hack because this is a DeFi / DEX protocol incident involving protocol control and collateral manipulation.

records.title: Drift suspended deposits and withdrawals
records.description: After confirming the active attack, Drift suspended deposits and withdrawals while coordinating containment with security firms, bridges, and exchanges.
records.notes: This is the state-changing event that justifies the limited classification pending final recovery or shutdown evidence.

records.title: Gemini announced UK, EEA, and Australia account closures
records.description: Gemini announced it would close customer accounts in the United Kingdom, European Economic Area, and Australia, with a phased regional wind-down ending on April 6, 2026.
records.notes: Gemini remains active globally; this is a regional-exit event, not a dead-side entity reclassification.

records.title: Gemini regional accounts entered withdrawal-only mode
records.description: Affected UK, EEA, and Australia customer accounts entered withdrawal-only handling before final regional closure.
records.notes: Australian fiat withdrawal handling had an earlier February 28 / March 1 transition, but HEI uses the broader March 5 marker for regional withdrawal-only handling.

records.title: Gemini closed customer accounts in selected regions
records.description: Gemini closed customer accounts in the UK, EEA, and Australia on April 6, 2026, while the global Gemini exchange entity remained active.
records.notes: Regional service termination only; Gemini entity status stays active.

records.title: ProBit Global announced full service termination
records.description: ProBit Global announced a complete service wind-down, citing a rapidly evolving regulatory environment and strategic business restructuring.
records.notes: The announcement date is taken from multiple mirrored / secondary references to the official notice.

records.title: ProBit Global spot trading ended
records.description: ProBit Global's shutdown roadmap ended spot trading and delisted all spot pairs on January 28, 2026.
records.notes: Time-zone conversions differ across syndicated reports, but the date-level marker is consistent.

records.title: ProBit Global standard services terminated
records.description: ProBit Global's standard platform services and normal withdrawal window ended on February 26, 2026, with later asset-return handling continuing into April.
records.notes: HEI uses 2026-02-26 as the terminal exchange-service marker while preserving the later asset-return deadline as a separate event.

records.title: ProBit Global delayed asset-return period ended
records.description: Reports of the ProBit Global wind-down describe a delayed asset-return period ending on April 1, 2026, after which unclaimed assets could be considered abandoned under the exchange terms.
records.notes: This is retained as post-shutdown custody / asset-return context rather than the core exchange-service terminal date.

records.title: Fastex Exchange service termination notice reported
records.description: A February 2026 report described a Fastex Exchange service-termination notice and withdrawal-only state attributed to MiCA / regulatory alignment.
records.notes: Month-level proxy date for the reported service-termination notice. Secondary reporting provides the regulatory / MiCA context; official-domain pages are attached to the effective halt event.

records.title: Fastex Exchange termination deadline reported
records.description: The reported service-termination notice set April 1, 2026 at 00:00 Yerevan Time as the terminal deadline for exchange operations.
records.notes: Official Fastex-domain pages state that exchange operations will halt on April 1 at 23:59 Yerevan time.

records.title: Grinex reported a major cyberattack
records.description: Grinex said it had suffered a large cyberattack that stole assets worth roughly 1 billion rubles, with outside estimates and blockchain analysis placing the loss around USD 13-15 million.
records.notes: Use hack rather than exploit because reporting describes a cyberattack on an exchange wallet / operations environment rather than a smart-contract exploit.

records.title: Grinex withdrawals and operations were suspended
records.description: Reporting described Grinex as suspending operations after the attack, with follow-on coverage describing halted trading and blocked withdrawals during the investigation.
records.notes: This is the direct status-changing event for the inactive classification.

records.title: Grinex was sanctioned by multiple jurisdictions
records.description: Reuters reported that Grinex had been sanctioned by the United States, United Kingdom, and European Union in 2025, with U.S. authorities linking it to sanctions-evasion infrastructure involving the A7A5 ruble-backed stablecoin.
records.notes: Year-level proxy event. This provides context and is not treated as the immediate cause of the April 2026 operational halt.

records.title: Rhea Finance suffered a major exploit
records.description: Rhea Finance suffered an exploit involving fake tokens, liquidity-pool manipulation, and oracle / validation or slippage-protection weakness; early reports estimated USD 7.6 million stolen, while later analysis described USD 18.4 million before recovery or freezes.
records.notes: Loss amount is intentionally described as a range / evolving estimate because early and later sources differ.

records.title: Rhea Finance withdrawals were halted
records.description: Reporting stated that withdrawals were halted while the Rhea team worked to contain further damage after the exploit.
records.notes: Keep limited until recovery or permanent shutdown evidence appears.

records.title: Bunni paused smart contracts after exploit
records.description: Bunni paused all smart-contract functions after an exploit drained an estimated USD 8.4 million across supported networks, including Ethereum and Unichain exposure.
records.notes: This is the initial crisis event that later led to the terminal shutdown.

records.title: Bunni announced shutdown after the exploit
records.description: Bunni announced it would shut down operations because the exploit left the team without enough resources to fund a secure relaunch, including audit and monitoring costs.
records.notes: Users could still withdraw assets, but protocol/business operations were being wound down.

records.title: Bunni treated as permanently closed
records.description: Follow-up reporting described Bunni as closing permanently after the exploit, with remaining funds to be distributed and contracts open-sourced.
records.notes: HEI uses 2025-10-23 as the terminal public reporting marker.

records.title: eXch cited law-enforcement pressure and laundering allegations
records.description: eXch said it had become the target of an active transatlantic operation amid allegations that Lazarus Group used the platform to launder funds from the Bybit hack; earlier reporting said eXch denied being a mixer but acknowledged processing a small portion of funds from the incident.
records.notes: Keep this as regulatory / law-enforcement-pressure context, not a finding of guilt.

records.title: eXch announced it would cease operations
records.description: eXch announced that it would cease operations on May 1, 2025, describing a management decision to cease and retreat in the hostile legal and surveillance environment.
records.notes: Announcement date follows Cointelegraph reporting of the April 17 notice.

records.title: eXch shutdown date reached
records.description: The announced eXch shutdown date was May 1, 2025, marking the terminal date used by HEI for the exchange entity.
records.notes: Use as terminal date unless archived official material later gives a more precise execution marker.

records.title: BigONE suffered a USD 27 million breach
records.description: BigONE confirmed a roughly USD 27 million hot-wallet / supply-chain breach, while stating that private keys and cold storage remained safe and that user balances would be fully covered.
records.notes: Not a death event; track as a major negative operational event.

records.title: BigONE deposits were temporarily suspended
records.description: BigONE temporarily suspended deposits while reinforcing system security after the breach, with later updates indicating deposit and trading services were restored.
records.notes: Temporary suspension only; does not change the entity status to dead or inactive.

records.title: BigONE trading was temporarily halted
records.description: Reporting described BigONE as temporarily freezing or suspending trading functions after the USD 27 million security incident while working with SlowMist to trace stolen funds.
records.notes: Temporary halt; keep entity active unless later evidence shows unresolved service failure.

records.title: Balancer v2 pools suffered a major exploit
records.description: Balancer v2 pools were exploited across multiple chains in November 2025, with secondary reports describing approximately USD 128 million drained and the flaw later cited as a source of legal exposure for Balancer Labs.
records.notes: Use exploit rather than hack because reporting attributes the loss to a protocol-level flaw in Balancer v2 logic.

records.title: Balancer Labs announced it would wind down
records.description: Balancer co-founder Fernando Martinelli announced that Balancer Labs would wind down, while explicitly stating that he was not calling for a full Balancer protocol shutdown and supported a lean continuation path.
records.notes: Corporate entity wind-down only. Do not treat as protocol death.

records.title: Polynomial Protocol announced final wind-down
records.description: Polynomial announced the closure of Polynomial Chain and Polynomial Trade, cancellation of its planned Q1 2026 TGE, and a phased wind-down including forced liquidation, liquidity-layer closure, and chain shutdown.
records.notes: Announcement date follows syndicated PANews / exchange-news reporting.

records.title: Polynomial forced-liquidation phase began
records.description: Polynomial's wind-down timeline scheduled forced liquidations for February 18, 2026 after operations ceased on February 14.
records.notes: Used as the trading/position termination marker within the wind-down sequence.

records.title: Polynomial Chain shutdown date reached
records.description: The final timeline for Polynomial's wind-down scheduled chain operations to halt on March 3, 2026, after liquidity-layer closure on February 24.
records.notes: HEI uses the chain-shutdown date as the terminal marker for the protocol/trading stack.

records.title: Step Finance suffered a major treasury breach
records.description: Step Finance suffered a treasury-wallet breach in late January 2026. Reports place the loss in a broad USD 26-40 million range depending on source and accounting for recovered or affected Remora assets.
records.notes: Loss amounts vary; avoid a single definitive figure in entity-level fields.

records.title: Step Finance announced group wind-down
records.description: Step Finance announced that Step Finance, SolanaFloor, and Remora Markets would wind down after failing to secure financing, acquisition, or another viable recovery path following the breach.
records.notes: Announcement date follows the project statement as reported by The Block, Cointelegraph, and other sources.

records.title: Step Finance treated as closed
records.description: Because the wind-down was described as immediate cessation of operations, HEI uses the announcement date as the effective closure marker for the Step Finance / Remora Markets group record.
records.notes: User recovery / buyback processes may continue after the platform operation marker.

records.title: Cetus Protocol suffered a major exploit
records.description: Cetus Protocol suffered a major exploit on May 22, 2025, with reports estimating about USD 223 million to USD 260 million drained from Sui/Aptos liquidity pools and more than USD 160 million of assets later frozen or recovered through the Sui ecosystem response.
records.notes: Loss amount varies across sources; use approximate language.

records.title: Cetus halted trading and paused contracts
records.description: Cetus halted trading / paused smart-contract activity while investigating the exploit and coordinating recovery through Sui validators, the Sui Foundation, and security partners.
records.notes: Temporary platform halt; not a terminal shutdown.

records.title: Cetus relaunched after recovery plan
records.description: Cetus relaunched on June 8, 2025 after recovery work, external audits, frozen-fund transfer approval, a Sui Foundation loan, and liquidity restoration for affected pools.
records.notes: Modeled as other because HEI has no dedicated restart/relaunch enum.

records.title: KiloEx suffered a price-oracle exploit
records.description: KiloEx suffered an approximately USD 7 million price-oracle manipulation exploit across multiple chains, including Base, BNB Chain, and Taiko exposure.
records.notes: Use exploit because the incident exploited oracle pricing logic rather than a general exchange-wallet breach.

records.title: KiloEx operations were paused after the exploit
records.description: KiloEx paused operations / trading for roughly ten days while preparing recovery and compensation measures after the oracle-manipulation attack.
records.notes: Temporary service disruption; not a shutdown.

records.title: KiloEx announced compensation and resolution plan
records.description: KiloEx published a resolution plan to compensate users with open positions and address trading-position imbalances before service resumption.
records.notes: Compensation/resolution context supporting active rather than dead classification.

records.title: Bybit announced Japan resident service restrictions
records.description: Bybit announced that it would discontinue services for Japanese residents and gradually apply account restrictions from 2026 in response to Japanese regulatory-compliance requirements, after earlier suspension of new Japan registrations.
records.notes: Regional exit only; Bybit global entity remains active.

records.title: Bybit Japan user verification deadline reached
records.description: Affected users were instructed to complete Identity Verification Level 2 / proof-of-address checks by January 22, 2026 or be classified as Japanese residents and face service limitations.
records.notes: Modeled as other because this is a regional KYC / residency-classification milestone rather than a global exchange status event.

records.title: OKX paused its DEX aggregator after Lazarus misuse concerns
records.description: OKX temporarily suspended its decentralized exchange aggregator after consulting regulators, citing efforts to prevent further misuse by Lazarus-linked activity and to apply security upgrades during heightened EU/MiCA scrutiny of its Web3 service.
records.notes: Product/service-level event only. OKX global exchange remains active.

records.title: OKX wallet service remained available while new wallet creation was limited in some markets
records.description: Reporting stated that OKX Wallet remained available to customers while new wallet creation was paused in selected markets as part of the service-review and upgrade response.
records.notes: Supporting service-scope event; not a full wallet or exchange shutdown.

records.title: Paradex pricing glitch triggered mass liquidations
records.description: A database migration / pricing-data error on Paradex briefly priced BTC at zero during maintenance, triggering mass unintended liquidations across perpetual markets.
records.notes: Use trading_halted because the incident affected trading, liquidation, and platform state.

records.title: Paradex performed a chain rollback
records.description: Paradex rolled back chain state to a prior block after the pricing-data incident, a rare and controversial response to a technical failure in a decentralized perpetuals platform.
records.notes: Modeled as other because HEI has no rollback-specific event enum.

records.title: Paradex refunded affected users after the rollback incident
records.description: Paradex said it refunded about USD 650,000 to around 200 users after the maintenance-related bug and unintended liquidations.
records.notes: Refund context supports active classification despite the critical incident.

records.title: SEC settled charges against Mango Markets entities
records.description: The SEC filed settled charges against Mango DAO, Blockworks Foundation, and Mango Labs over unregistered offers/sales of MNGO tokens and unregistered broker activity connected to the Mango Markets platform.
records.notes: Primary regulatory precursor to the 2025 wind-down.

records.title: Mango Markets announced it would shut down
records.description: Mango Markets announced that users should close positions and that Mango v4 / Boost were winding down, with governance proposals set to make most borrowing economically unviable.
records.notes: Announcement date follows reporting of Mango's public January 11 statement.

records.title: Mango Markets wind-down proposals became executable
records.description: Governance proposals tied to the wind-down became executable on January 13, 2025, effectively ending core borrowing/lending/trading viability on Mango v4 / Boost.
records.notes: HEI uses the proposal execution date as the terminal operational marker.

records.title: Nobitex suffered a politically motivated hack
records.description: Nobitex was attacked by hackers identifying as Predatory Sparrow / Gonjeshke Darande, with roughly USD 90 million in crypto assets transferred to inaccessible or burned addresses and source-code exposure threatened.
records.notes: The event is politically motivated and destructive rather than normal theft for profit, according to multiple sources.

records.title: Nobitex website and app were taken offline
records.description: Nobitex said it pulled its website and app offline while reviewing unauthorized access to its systems and hot wallet infrastructure after the attack.
records.notes: Modeled as withdrawal_suspended because site/app offline and system review imply service interruption, but not enough for dead classification.

records.title: WazirX suffered a USD 230-235 million hack
records.description: WazirX suffered a large hack in July 2024 that drained roughly USD 230-235 million from the platform and led to a long suspension and restructuring process.
records.notes: Loss amount varies slightly by source; keep approximate language.

records.title: WazirX halted withdrawals and trading after the breach
records.description: Following the July 2024 breach, WazirX suspended platform operations including withdrawals and trading, producing a roughly 15-16 month interruption before phased restart.
records.notes: This is the main service-interruption marker.

records.title: Singapore High Court sanctioned WazirX restructuring scheme
records.description: Zettai announced that Singapore's High Court sanctioned a creditor-approved restructuring scheme, paving the way for WazirX to restart operations within ten business days of effectiveness.
records.notes: Modeled as other because HEI has no restructuring-approved enum.

records.title: WazirX restarted trading after long suspension
records.description: WazirX restarted trading on October 24, 2025, roughly 15-16 months after the July 2024 hack, with phased market activation and zero-fee restart offers.
records.notes: Restart marker supports active classification; some reports noted withdrawals remained constrained during early restart.

records.title: Phemex detected a hot-wallet security incident
records.description: Phemex detected unusual activity in its hot wallet and described the incident as a hacker attack, isolating affected devices and contacting security firms and law enforcement.
records.notes: Official source is primary; external estimates of loss are not required for entity classification.

records.title: Phemex temporarily suspended deposits and withdrawals
records.description: Phemex suspended deposits and withdrawals across chains as an emergency response to the hot-wallet incident, then resumed chains and assets in staged updates.
records.notes: Official timeline gives asset-by-asset restoration dates.

records.title: Phemex withdrawal services were restored
records.description: Phemex's final update stated that all withdrawal services were restored in February 2025 after the hot-wallet incident response and system security upgrades.
records.notes: Month-level restoration marker based on official final update.

records.title: Upbit suffered a Solana hot-wallet incident
records.description: Upbit detected abnormal withdrawals of Solana-network assets, with reporting estimating roughly USD 30-40 million or 54 billion KRW in assets transferred to an unauthorized external wallet.
records.notes: Loss figures vary by source and exchange update; keep approximate range.

records.title: Upbit suspended deposits and withdrawals after abnormal activity
records.description: Upbit urgently suspended digital-asset deposits and withdrawals after detecting abnormal activity involving Solana-network tokens and moved remaining assets to cold wallets.
records.notes: Temporary suspension; not a death event.

records.title: Upbit scheduled Solana-related deposit and withdrawal resumption
records.description: Upbit announced that deposits and withdrawals for Solana network-related digital assets and SOL staking services would resume sequentially from December 4, 2025.
records.notes: Restoration marker supports active classification.

records.title: CoinDCX confirmed an internal operational-account hack
records.description: CoinDCX confirmed a security breach involving an internal operational account, with reports estimating about USD 44 million stolen; the company said customer funds were not affected.
records.notes: Use careful wording: sources report that an internal operational account was affected, while customer funds were said to be safe.

records.title: CoinDCX paused or limited some services after the breach
records.description: Reports described temporary product/service restrictions after the breach, including Web3 / INR and crypto-withdrawal handling, while CoinDCX worked to contain the incident and reassure users about fund safety.
records.notes: Modeled as withdrawal_suspended because the service impact centered on transfer/withdrawal handling rather than a permanent exchange halt.

records.title: CoinDCX resumed withdrawals after the breach
records.description: Indian business press reported that CoinDCX resumed withdrawals after the USD 44 million breach and reiterated that user funds were safe.
records.notes: Restoration marker supports active classification.

records.title: Bybit suffered a record-scale Ethereum wallet hack
records.description: Bybit suffered a record-scale Ethereum wallet hack in February 2025, with reports estimating about USD 1.4-1.5 billion stolen from a cold-wallet transfer/signing process while the exchange continued operating globally.
