records.title: CoinEx agreed to New York ban and monetary settlement
records.description: CoinEx agreed to pay about $1.8 million and be banned from operating in New York to resolve a lawsuit brought by the New York Attorney General alleging illegal unregistered operations in the state.
records.notes: This is a jurisdiction-specific regulatory action and does not mark CoinEx as dead globally.

records.title: CoinEx reported hot-wallet security breach
records.description: CoinEx reported a hot-wallet security incident in September 2023; third-party reporting and summaries described losses of roughly $70 million and suspected Lazarus Group attribution by blockchain analysts.
records.notes: Security breach details should be strengthened later with primary CoinEx incident posts or archived forensic reports if available.

records.title: CoinEx verified as active exchange candidate
records.description: CoinEx is consolidated from CoinPaprika and CoinGecko candidate rows into one active centralized exchange record.
records.notes: Consumes database candidate rows hei_unadded_0550 and hei_unadded_0551.

records.title: Coinone entered the South Korean exchange market
records.description: Coinone began operating in South Korea's virtual-currency exchange market in October 2014.
records.notes: Month-level launch marker based on Coinone's official retrospective notice; the exact launch day is not asserted.

records.title: Coins.ph launched
records.description: Coins.ph launched in the Philippines in January 2014 as a cryptocurrency and exchange platform.
records.notes: Month-level launch marker based on the official about page.

records.title: CommEX became the destination for Binance Russia business transition
records.description: Binance announced that it had agreed to sell its Russian business to CommEX and would gradually wind down exchange services and other Russian business lines while migrating users to the CommEX platform.
records.notes: Used as the public launch/market-entry marker because CommEX was publicly identified through the Binance Russia business transition.

records.title: CommEX announced staged service suspension
records.description: CommEX announced that operations would begin suspending on 2024-03-25 and that access to the website would fully close on 2024-05-10.
records.notes: Used as the operator-announced shutdown marker.

records.title: CommEX website access scheduled to close
records.description: CommEX's staged closure schedule set 2024-05-10 as the full website-access closure date, making it the terminal exchange-service marker for HEI.
records.notes: Used as the death_date because the publicly reported closure schedule named this date for full access closure.

records.title: CoW Swap launched on Ethereum mainnet
records.description: CoW Swap was publicly announced and deployed to Ethereum mainnet as a production proof of concept for CoW Protocol's batch-auction and solver-based trading mechanism.
records.notes: Exact launch date comes from CoW DAO's official anniversary chronology.

records.title: CoW Swap's cow.fi domain was temporarily taken over
records.description: Control of the cow.fi domain was transferred without authorization for several hours and an unsafe frontend was served. CoW DAO stated that the incident affected the domain and frontend rather than the underlying protocol systems.
records.notes: Classified as hack because the official domain was taken over; the underlying protocol remained operational.

records.title: Crema Finance exploit reported
records.description: Crema Finance was reported to have suffered a smart-contract / flash-loan-related exploit in July 2022, with public reporting describing roughly $9 million affected.
records.notes: DOJ materials describe an unnamed Solana DEX; Axios identifies clues pointing to Crema Finance, and CoinDesk reports on the Crema Finance exploit and partial return of funds.

records.title: Attacker return and bounty arrangement reported
records.description: CoinDesk reported that the attacker returned almost $8 million to Crema Finance while retaining about $1.7 million as a bounty arrangement.
records.notes: This event records public reporting of the recovery/bounty arrangement, not an endorsement of its legal effect.

records.title: Crema Finance consolidated as DEX candidate
records.description: Crema Finance is consolidated from the DefiLlama candidate row into one DEX / protocol record.
records.notes: Consumes verified-unadded row hei_unadded_0600.

records.title: Curve entered public operation
records.description: Curve began operating as an Ethereum-based decentralized exchange for stable-value and correlated assets.
records.notes: Approximate month-level launch marker.

records.title: Coinbase announced agreement to acquire Deribit
records.description: Coinbase announced an agreement to acquire Deribit in a transaction reported at about $2.9 billion, expanding Coinbase into crypto derivatives and options markets.
records.notes: This event records the acquisition announcement / agreement. It does not by itself mark Deribit as shut down.

records.title: Deribit consolidated as active exchange entity
records.description: Deribit and Deribit Spot candidate rows are consolidated into one active centralized derivatives exchange entity.
records.notes: Consumes verified-unadded rows hei_unadded_0707 and hei_unadded_0708.

records.title: DODO V1 emerged as the protocol's first DEX product
records.description: DODO V1 entered operation in August 2020 as the first version of the protocol, introducing its Proactive Market Maker approach to decentralized liquidity and trading.
records.notes: Month-level launch marker; no exact day is asserted.

records.title: Several DODO V2 Crowdpool pools were affected by a security incident
records.description: Several DODO V2 Crowdpool pools were affected by a March 2021 security incident. Public incident analyses and partner updates stated that other V1 and non-Crowdpool V2 pools were not affected, and recovery work followed.
records.notes: The incident affected a subset of V2 Crowdpool pools and did not terminate the wider DODO protocol.

records.title: dYdX launched its first decentralized perpetual market
records.description: dYdX launched a BTC-USDC perpetual contract market on Ethereum on 2020-04-20, marking the protocol's first production decentralized perpetual exchange product and the operational start used by HEI for the exchange entity.
records.notes: Earlier lending and margin products existed, but HEI uses the first perpetual exchange launch as the cleanest exchange start marker.

records.title: dYdX Chain mainnet launched and protocol migration began
records.description: The community-launched dYdX Chain mainnet went live on 2023-10-26 using the Cosmos SDK and CometBFT. Markets initially operated in post-only mode, and governance enabled full-production trading in November 2023 as the ecosystem migrated away from the Ethereum/StarkEx v3 deployment.
records.notes: Classified as token_migration because the transition included both protocol deployment migration and DYDX token migration to the new chain.

records.title: EtherDelta operated as an early Ethereum token exchange
records.description: EtherDelta operated as an Ethereum-based token trading venue before the SEC's later regulatory action involving its founder.
records.notes: Launch/public-operation date is approximate and based on regulator and database context.

records.title: SEC action involving EtherDelta founder
records.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission announced a settled action involving EtherDelta's founder and the operation of EtherDelta as an online trading platform for ERC20 tokens.
records.notes: This is a founder/operator regulatory action and does not by itself provide a precise platform shutdown date.

records.title: EtherDelta consolidated from backlog candidate
records.description: EtherDelta is consolidated from the CoinPaprika candidate row into one historical DEX record.
records.notes: Consumes verified-unadded row hei_unadded_0843.

records.title: Fairdesk launched
records.description: Fairdesk's closure announcement described the exchange as officially launched in 2021 and offering spot and futures cryptocurrency trading.
records.notes: Only the year is established by the available operator announcement; 2021-01-01 is a schema-normalized year-level date.

records.title: Fairdesk announced permanent closure
records.description: Fairdesk announced that all futures and spot positions would close on 2024-10-17, that only withdrawals would remain during the wind-down, and that the platform would permanently close on 2024-11-30.
records.notes: The operator cited evolving market conditions and regulatory changes.

records.title: Fairdesk reached its announced final closure date
records.description: Fairdesk's published wind-down schedule set 2024-11-30 as the permanent closure date after a withdrawal-only period.
records.notes: Used as the terminal marker according to the operator's formal closure schedule.

records.title: Foxbit was founded
records.description: Foxbit was founded in Brazil on 2014-12-10 as a cryptocurrency exchange.
records.notes: Exact founding date is stated in Foxbit's official anniversary history.

records.title: Garantex began operating
records.description: Garantex operated as a Russia-linked centralized cryptocurrency exchange from 2019.
records.notes: Month-level launch date based on public background references.

records.title: U.S. Treasury sanctioned Garantex
records.description: The U.S. Treasury sanctioned Garantex as part of an action against Russia-based Hydra Market and a ransomware-enabling virtual currency exchange.
records.notes: Regulatory action predating the 2025 service collapse.

records.title: Services suspended after sanctions and wallet blocking
records.description: Garantex suspended services after Tether blocked wallets on the platform, with international enforcement activity and web-infrastructure seizure following in the same period.
records.notes: Used as death_date because the platform stopped providing services during the sanctions and seizure period.

records.title: Gate.io began operating
records.description: Gate.io traces its exchange operation to 2013 and remains active under the Gate brand.
records.notes: Year-level launch marker based on official company history.

records.title: gTrade entered operation
records.description: Gains Network began operating gTrade as a decentralized leveraged-trading and perpetuals exchange.
records.notes: Year-level launch marker.

records.title: HKVAEX launched its Hong Kong trading platform
records.description: BX Services Limited launched HKVAEX in February 2023.
records.notes: Month-level launch precision.

records.title: HKVAEX withdrew its VATP licence application
records.description: The Hong Kong SFC removed HKVAEX from the applicant list after withdrawal.

records.title: HKVAEX completed its phased shutdown
records.description: Trading and deposits ended during April and the website closed on 2024-05-01.

records.title: Huobi exchange entered operation
records.description: The exchange now known as HTX entered operation under the Huobi brand in September 2013.
records.notes: Month-level launch marker; the later HTX rebrand remains part of the same continuing entity.

records.title: Huobi Korea was established as Huobi's Korean exchange
records.description: Huobi Korea began operating in 2017 as the South Korean arm of the global Huobi exchange group.
records.notes: Year-level launch precision is retained because the current source set establishes 2017 but not a precise day.

records.title: Huobi Korea separated from the global Huobi and HTX business
records.description: Huobi Korea cut its ownership and management ties with Huobi Global and HTX and continued as an independent domestic exchange.
records.notes: Modeled as an identity transition because ownership independence materially changed the exchange's relationship to the global Huobi lineage.

records.title: Huobi Korea ended virtual-asset exchange services
records.description: Huobi Korea terminated its exchange services on 2024-01-29 after concluding that the current business environment made continued operation impractical. Customer withdrawals remained available after the shutdown.

records.title: Indodax was established
records.description: Indodax was officially established in Indonesia on 2014-02-15 as a cryptocurrency trading platform.
records.notes: Exact establishment date is stated in official Indodax materials.

records.title: Jupiter launched as a Solana liquidity-routing exchange
records.description: Jupiter entered public operation as a Solana swap-routing and liquidity-aggregation venue and later expanded into a broader decentralized trading platform.
records.notes: Month-level launch marker; no exact day is asserted.

records.title: Kraken was founded
records.description: Kraken traces its founding to 2011-07-28, when the exchange project began under the Kraken name.
records.notes: The event uses Kraken’s official founding date already stored in the canonical entity.

records.title: KuCoin began operating in 2017
records.description: KuCoin was founded and began operating as a centralized cryptocurrency exchange in 2017.
records.notes: The canonical entity stores the launch at year precision; this event preserves that precision.

records.title: KuCoin disclosed a major hot-wallet security breach
records.description: KuCoin disclosed that private keys associated with hot wallets had been compromised and that large quantities of crypto assets were transferred out of the exchange. The exchange remained active and later reported substantial recovery and insurance coverage.
records.notes: The incident did not produce a terminal status change.

records.title: KuCoin pleaded guilty in a U.S. unlicensed money-transmission case
records.description: Peken Global Ltd, operating as KuCoin, pleaded guilty to operating an unlicensed money-transmitting business, agreed to more than $297 million in fines and forfeiture, and accepted a two-year exit from the U.S. market.
records.notes: The action was jurisdiction-specific and did not end KuCoin's global exchange operation.

records.title: KUNA launched in Ukraine
records.description: KUNA operated as a Ukrainian cryptocurrency exchange and became one of the country's best-known local trading platforms.
records.notes: Year-level launch date based on public background references.

records.title: Closure process began after domestic blocking and investigation context
records.description: KUNA's founder announced that the exchange would wind down commercial operations after domestic blocking connected to Ukrainian enforcement and court action.
records.notes: Regulatory/legal trigger event preceding the final shutdown date.

records.title: KUNA completed platform shutdown
records.description: KUNA's closure roadmap set April 30, 2025 as the complete cessation date for the platform after earlier deposit, exchange, and withdrawal deadlines.
records.notes: Used as death_date because public closure reporting identifies this as the complete cessation date.

records.title: KyberSwap entered public operation
records.description: Kyber Network published a user guide for the newly launched KyberSwap interface in August 2018, providing an early public operational marker for the exchange product.
records.notes: The marker follows contemporaneous official KyberSwap materials rather than a later DMM rebrand.

records.title: KyberSwap Elastic liquidity pools were affected by a security incident
records.description: A November 2023 security incident affected KyberSwap Elastic liquidity pools. KyberSwap later published a postmortem and established a treasury grant program for eligible affected users.
records.notes: The incident affected Elastic pools but did not terminate the wider KyberSwap service.

records.title: LBank began operating
records.description: LBank began operating as a centralized cryptocurrency exchange in 2015.
records.notes: Year-level launch marker based on official company history.

records.title: Trader Joe DEX launched on Avalanche
records.description: The exchange began operation on Avalanche under the Trader Joe brand and developed into a major liquidity and swap venue in the ecosystem.
records.notes: Month-level launch marker; no exact day is asserted.

records.title: Trader Joe adopted the LFJ brand
records.description: The continuing Trader Joe protocol identity adopted the LFJ name while retaining the JOE token, Liquidity Book technology and multichain exchange operation.
records.notes: Month-level rebrand marker. Modeled as an in-place brand change, not a terminal rebranded entity status.

records.title: BITMAX began operations in Japan
records.description: LVC Corporation announced that BITMAX had gone live for the Japanese market, initially through the Android version of the LINE app, after LVC registered as a cryptocurrency exchanger under Japan's Payment Services Act.
records.notes: The official LINE release provides the exact service-launch date and distinguishes BITMAX from the separate BITBOX service.

records.title: LINE Xenesis announced LINE BITMAX closure
records.description: LINE Xenesis announced that LINE BITMAX would end on 2026-06-01 and published withdrawal, asset-conversion, refund, and custody procedures for customers.
records.notes: The announcement states that the operator would also discontinue its crypto-asset exchange business.

records.title: LINE BITMAX service ended
records.description: LINE BITMAX reached its announced termination date. The official service site and help center subsequently displayed that the service had ended on 2026-06-01.
records.notes: Used as the terminal operational marker.

records.title: Luno was founded
records.description: Luno was founded in 2013 and developed consumer cryptocurrency products for international markets.
records.notes: Year-level launch marker based on official company history.

records.title: 0x launched the Matcha DEX aggregator
records.description: The 0x team released Matcha as a user-facing decentralized exchange aggregator designed to search and split trades across multiple liquidity sources.
records.notes: Exact date follows Matcha's official launch announcement.

records.title: Mercado Bitcoin began operating in 2013
records.description: Mercado Bitcoin began operating in Brazil in 2013 as a cryptocurrency trading platform.
records.notes: Year-level launch marker based on official company history.

records.title: MEXC began operating
records.description: MEXC began operating as a centralized cryptocurrency exchange in 2018.
records.notes: Year-level launch marker because the official company page states the operating year without an exact public day.

records.title: 9inch present in exchange source data
records.description: 9inch appeared in the verified-unadded candidate generator from CoinPaprika and CoinGecko source data as an exchange-like DEX candidate not found in current HEI records.
records.notes: This is a database-reference event, not a verified launch event. Replace with a proper launch/history event if stronger sources are added.

records.title: 9mm present in exchange and DEX source data
records.description: 9mm appeared in the verified-unadded candidate generator from CoinPaprika, CoinGecko, and DefiLlama source data as an exchange-like DEX candidate not found in current HEI records.
records.notes: This is a database-reference event, not a verified launch event. Replace with a proper launch/history event if stronger sources are added.

records.title: 1DEX present in exchange and DEX source data
records.description: 1DEX appeared in the verified-unadded candidate generator from CoinGecko and DefiLlama source data as an exchange-like DEX candidate not found in current HEI records.
records.notes: This is a database-reference event, not a verified launch event. Replace with a proper launch/history event if stronger sources are added.

records.title: 1inch DEX aggregator launched
records.description: 1inch emerged as a decentralized exchange aggregator for routing token swaps across multiple liquidity sources. HEI stores May 2019 as the approximate origin month pending stronger official date evidence.
records.notes: Launch timing is approximate and should be refined if a stronger official date source is added.

records.title: Orca entered public operation
records.description: Orca began operating as a decentralized exchange on Solana.
records.notes: Approximate month-level launch marker.

records.title: Osmosis mainnet launched
records.description: Osmosis launched its app-chain decentralized exchange and liquidity network in the Cosmos ecosystem.
records.notes: Mainnet launch marker.

records.title: Paribu launched for trading
records.description: Paribu launched its cryptocurrency trading platform in Turkey on 2017-02-14.
records.notes: Exact launch date is stated in multiple official Paribu histories.

records.title: ProBit Korea opened its Korean-won service
records.description: The domestic fiat exchange opened for Korean-won deposits.

records.title: ProBit Korea announced closure
records.description: The operator announced that exchange services would end on 2024-03-16.

records.title: ProBit Korea ended exchange services
records.description: Exchange services ended and remaining users were directed to an asset-return process.

records.title: QuickSwap launched
records.description: QuickSwap launched as a decentralized exchange for the Polygon ecosystem.
records.notes: Month-level launch date from official documentation.

records.title: Raydium began operating as a Solana AMM and DEX
records.description: Raydium began operation in 2021 as an automated market maker and decentralized exchange integrated with Solana's order-book liquidity infrastructure.
records.notes: Year-level marker; no exact day is asserted.

records.title: Compromised pool authority enabled a Raydium liquidity-pool exploit
records.description: A malicious actor gained control of Raydium Liquidity Pool V4's pool-owner authority and withdrew assets through fee-accounting instructions. Raydium revoked the compromised authority, deployed a mitigation and later moved authority controls to multisig infrastructure.
records.notes: This incident did not terminate the broader Raydium protocol.

records.title: Serum DEX went live on Solana
records.description: Serum launched as a non-custodial decentralized exchange and on-chain central-limit-order-book protocol on Solana. Project Serum later described the protocol as having gone live in August 2020, while contemporaneous launch reporting dated the live deployment to 2020-08-30.
records.notes: The exact day follows contemporaneous launch reporting; the project itself confirms the August 2020 launch month.

records.title: FTX security incident made Serum program control unsafe
records.description: After unauthorized activity affected FTX, Solana ecosystem developers concluded that Serum's FTX-controlled upgrade authority could no longer be trusted. Developers copied Serum v3 into a newly deployed, multisig-controlled program that became OpenBook, while major integrations moved away from the original Serum program.
records.notes: The event is classified as exploit because the terminal security response followed the FTX incident and loss of confidence in the Serum upgrade authority. No direct loss from the Serum order book is asserted.

records.title: Project Serum declared the original mainnet program defunct
records.description: Project Serum stated that the collapse of Alameda and FTX had rendered the original Serum mainnet program defunct. It pointed users and protocols toward the community-led OpenBook fork, noting that Serum liquidity and volume had fallen to near zero and that the old program carried security risk.
records.notes: Used as the terminal date because the project's own account explicitly described the original mainnet program as defunct.

records.title: Tapp Exchange launched on Aptos
records.description: Contemporary shutdown reporting states that Tapp Exchange launched in June 2025 as a V4-style decentralized exchange in the Aptos ecosystem.
records.notes: Month-level launch date normalized to the first day of June 2025 because an exact public launch day was not established.

records.title: Tapp Exchange announced a phased protocol shutdown
records.description: Tapp Exchange announced that it had begun winding down the protocol. The team said normal access and withdrawals would remain available through 2026-05-31 and asked users to remove assets before the frontend was retired.
records.notes: Multiple contemporaneous reports reproduce the operator's May 1 shutdown statement and withdrawal instructions.

records.title: Tapp Exchange reached its frontend shutdown date
records.description: The published wind-down schedule set 2026-05-31 as the end of normal protocol access and the date the Tapp Exchange frontend would be taken offline. After that point, remaining withdrawals were expected to require direct smart-contract interaction.
records.notes: Used as the terminal operated-service marker even though underlying on-chain contracts may remain technically callable.

records.title: THORChain multichain Chaosnet launched
records.description: THORChain launched multichain Chaosnet on 2021-04-13 with a capped RUNE allocation, enabling native cross-chain liquidity and swaps across supported assets without wrapping them into one chain.
records.notes: Official development update provides the exact launch date.

records.title: Ethereum router exploits halted THORChain trading
records.description: THORChain suffered two Ethereum-router exploits in July 2021. The incidents drained assets from the protocol, prompted trading halts and reimbursement commitments, and led to a security-hardening program before the network was progressively restored.
records.notes: The representative date is the first widely reported July exploit. The event description covers the linked July 2021 exploit sequence documented in THORChain's own postmortem.

records.title: WhiteBIT was founded
records.description: WhiteBIT was founded in 2018 as a centralized cryptocurrency exchange.
records.notes: Year-level launch marker based on official company history.

records.title: Zedxion's UK corporate exchange lineage was established
records.description: Zedxion Exchange Ltd was incorporated in the United Kingdom on 2021-05-17. OFAC later identified it as a UK-registered digital-asset exchange and used the same establishment date in its designation record.
records.notes: This is a corporate-registration marker used as the earliest authoritative lineage date, not a claim that trading necessarily opened on the same day.

records.title: OFAC designated Zedxion Exchange Ltd
records.description: The U.S. Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control designated Zedxion Exchange Ltd under Iran-sector and counterterrorism authorities. Treasury stated that Zedxion-linked addresses had processed funds for wallets linked to the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps and designated the exchange for operating in Iran's financial sector and materially supporting the IRGC.
records.notes: The designation subjected the named UK company and listed exchange identity to U.S. sanctions and secondary-sanctions risk.

records.title: Companies House dissolved Zedxion Exchange Ltd
records.description: Companies House recorded the final compulsory strike-off and dissolution of Zedxion Exchange Ltd on 2026-05-12. The registrar stated that the action related to incorporation information or statements considered misleading, false, or deceptive. The public exchange website nevertheless remained accessible after the dissolution date, so HEI records a limited rather than terminal dead-side status.
records.notes: Corporate dissolution is established, but operational shutdown is not. The distinction is preserved explicitly.

records.title: 0x Protocol present in DEX aggregation source data

records.notes: This is a database-reference event, not a verified launch event. Replace with a proper launch/history event if stronger sources are added.
